course_title: Management of Adult Voice and Fluency Disorders
course_description:
This course provides students with theoretical knowledge and clinical skills in the assessment and management of voice and fluency disorders in adults. Students will develop a comprehensive understanding of normal voice production, vocal pathology that may disrupt vocal function, and evidence-based approaches for diagnosis and intervention of voice disorders in adults. The course also explores the nature, aetiology, and characteristics of fluency disorders in adults, with an emphasis on differential diagnosis and evidence-based treatment approaches.

Through a combination of lectures, case studies, and practical tutorials, students will gain competencies in:

· Conducting comprehensive voice and fluency assessments

· Analysing normal and disordered voice characteristics and fluency behaviours

· Applying evidence-based treatment techniques to a range of voice and fluency disorders

· Understanding the psychosocial impacts of voice and fluency disorders on clients and their families

By the end of this course, students will possess the professional knowledge and clinical skills necessary to effectively assess and treat adults with voice and fluency disorders.

Additional timetable information
A course outline in relation to teaching activities and assessment will provided on the SPCH7234 Blackboard site.
course_aims: This course aims to provide students with foundation knowledge relating to voice and fluency disorders in adults and to develop professional competencies in the assessment, diagnosis and clinical management of these disorders. [SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.3-1.6, 2.5, 2.6, 3.2-3.5]

learning_outcomes.description: Critically analyse and synthesise theoretical knowledge related to normal and disordered voice and fluency in adults. (SPA PS Standards 1.1, 1.2)

Conduct comprehensive assessments of adult voice and fluency disorders, incorporating observational, perceptual, and psychosocial measures within an ICF framework to support differential diagnosis and treatment planning. (SPA PS Standards 1.5, 3.1, 3.2)

Formulate and implement individualised, evidence-based intervention plans for adult voice and fluency disorders, drawing on advanced clinical reasoning and multiple theoretical approaches. (SPA PS Standards 1.6, 1.7, 3.3, 3.4)
Adapt and evaluate therapy approaches to support effective, sustainable implementation, including home practice, outcome monitoring, and responsiveness to change. (SPA PS Standards 1.6, 3.4, 3.5)
Demonstrate person- and family-centred care , using collaborative goal setting and shared decision-making in assessment and intervention planning. (SPA PS Standards 1.4, 3.4, 3.5)

Deliver culturally safe and responsive care , designing and evaluating interventions that respect and affirm cultural, linguistic, and gender diversity, including the needs of transgender and gender-diverse individuals. (SPA PS Standards 1.6, 3.4, 3.5)

Demonstrate readiness for professional practice in adult voice and fluency , integrating clinical reasoning, ethical conduct, and practical skills in simulated settings (e.g., OSCEs and case-based simulations). (SPA PS Standards 1.3, 1.4, 3.3, 3.5, 4.1)

assessment_details.title: Knowledge Check Quizzes
assessment_details.due_date: Week 1 content 10/12/2025 1:00 pm Week 2 content 17/12/2025 1:00 pm Week 3 content 24/12/2025 1:00 pm Week 4 content 7/01/2026 1:00 pm Week 5 content 14/01/2026 1:00 pm Week 6 content 21/01/2026 1:00 pm Week 8 content 11/02/2026 1:00 pm All quizzes will contain multiple choice questions.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: There will be a series of seven (7) weekly knowledge check quizzes in this course. Your five (5) highest scoring quizzes will make up your grade (2% each).The two (2) quizzes that you receive the lowest marks for (which may include any quizzes that are not submitted) will not contribute to your grade. Quizzes will cover content from weeks 1-6 and 8. Each quiz opens during the content week but is due the following Wednesday at 1pm (e.g., the Week 1 quiz opens in Week 1 and is due Wednesday of Week 2 by 1pm). *Please note, the final quiz is due on the Wednesday of the end of semester exam period. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
All quizzes will be undertaken through an assessment link provided in Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Because you are permitted to exclude two (2) quizzes out of seven (7) that you received the lowest scores on, no extensions or deferrals are available for the quizzes.
Students with a disability, mental health or medical condition, illness, injury or exceptional circumstances are strongly encouraged to make an appointment with a student adviser as soon as possible, in order to determine appropriate arrangements for reasonable adjustments.
Late submission
You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.

assessment_details.title: In-semester exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This is a MUST PASS examination. This in-semester exam will assess knowledge and skills acquired from weekly pre-recorded lectures and tutorials in relation to the assessment, diagnosis, and management of voice and fluency disorders (Weeks 1-5). This assessment will encompass short answer questions including case-based questions relating to assessment interpretation, differential diagnosis and management options for voice and fluency disorders. Students will also be required to perform an online assessment of stuttering behaviours and vocal features from a video recording during this exam. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time 10 minutes Duration 90 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Closed book examination - no written materials permitted Exam platform Inspera Invigilation Invigilated in person
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Deferred exams can be requested for this assessment item. Please apply for a deferred exam if there are exceptional or unavoidable circumstances, or as a one-off discretionary request. Deferred exam requests should be submitted as soon as possible, and no later than five calendar days after the date of the original exam. Further details, including how to apply, can be found at - Deferring an exam. Please also email the Course Coordinator to advise of the deferred exam request.

assessment_details.title: Voice/ Fluency Objective Structured Clinical Examination (OSCE)
assessment_details.category: Examination, Practical/ Demonstration, Role play/ Simulation
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This Objective Structured Clinical Examination (OSCE) is comprised of two stations: 1) adult with a voice disorder and 2) adult with a fluency disorder. Prior to the commencement of each OSCE, you will receive case information relevant to the case. You will have 15 minutes to review the case information and plan for the session. You will then enter the clinic room and meet your client. You will have 15 minutes to conduct each session where you will: a. Provide a brief outline of the session b. Summarise findings from initial assessment session/ obtain a speech sample and identify stuttering behaviours c. Provide education regarding the presenting disorder and rationale for chosen treatment d. Implement chosen treatment technique and provide feedback to client as required e. Conclude the session You will then rotate to the next station to complete the second OSCE. This assessment task is to be completed inperson. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time 14 minutes Duration 30 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Closed book examination - specified written materials permitted Exam platform Other Invigilation Invigilated in person
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 8
learning_activities.topic: Online self-directed learning Pre-recorded lecture content will be available on Blackboard across weeks 1-8. It is expected that students will engage with this content prior to attending the weekly tutorials. Up to four (4) hours of pre-recorded lecture material (voice and fluency content combined) will be provided on the course Blackboard site each week. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 8
learning_activities.topic: Weekly on-campus tutorials Weekly tutorials (2 x 2 hour tutorials) will be held in weeks 1-3 and 5-8 of semester. Tutorials will be practically-based and will facilitate student understanding and application of the course content. Week 7 tutorials will focus on OSCE preparation in readiness for end of semester practical assessment. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
